The 2024 Paris Olympics concluded on Friday with Team USA leading the total medal count with 111 medals, including a leading 33 gold.

Covered by:Paulina DedajandJackson Thompson

Coverage for this event has ended.

Former Eagles center Jason Kelce is in Paris for the Olympics with his wife Kylie Kelce, he said during an appearance on "Green Light with Chris Long" on Friday.

The couple went to watch one of the field hockey games and Jason even arm-wrestled a rugby player.

Jason said his wife has impressed the locals with her ability to speak French, picking up after him.

"Every time she speaks at the other French people, the Parisians are like complimenting her on her accent or they think we're Canadian," Jason said. "So I think that that like helps us [not be seen as American]."

The coach of American sprinter Noah Lyles, Lance Brauman, said Lyles had a 102 degrees Fahrenheit fever when he won bronze in the men's 200-meter final on Thursday. After the race, it was announced that Lyles had test positive for COVID-19 the night before.

"Those guys raced great," Brauman said. "But to get a bronze medal in 19.70 with a temperature of about 102, that wasn't too bad."

Lyles, 27, was back at the track Friday night wearing a protective mask while accepting his bronze medal. He did a lap around the track waving to fans but kept his distance from Tebogo and Bednarek.

The Associated Press contributed to this report.



The2024 Paris Olympicsis entering the final stretch as the Americans continued to lead the overall medal count with 111 medals.

Day 14 of competition saw theU.S. women’s 4x100-meter relay teamreclaim gold in the final thanks to American track star Sha'Carri Richardson. Twanisha Terry picked up her first medal of the Games with the win and Gabrielle Thomas and Melissa Jefferson earned their second.

American hurdler Raj Benjamin continued that trend in the men’s 400-meter hurdles final when he defeated reigning Olympic champion Karsten Warholm of Norway to earn his first gold medal.

Olivia Reeves also notched a historic win in the women’s 71-kilogram weightlifting division, earning America’s first gold medal in the event since the 2000 Sydney Olympics.

The final medal count of Team USA includes a leading 33 gold medals and 39 silver and bronze. Only China comes close, tying the gold medal count.

American hurdler Raj Benjamin won gold in the 400-meter hurdle final on Friday.

Benjamin defeated reigning Olympic champion Karsten Warholm of Norway to win the 400-meter hurdles gold.

For Bejamin, the gold marks a resilient comeback from the Tokyo Olympics, where he lost to Warholm despite running a then-personal-best of 46.17, shy of Warholm's 45.94 to win gold.

At the 2023 Diamond League, ran the fourth-fastest 400m hurdles ever recorded, clocking a time of 46.39 seconds to secure the title and beat world record-holder Warholm for only the second time in his career. Now he has beaten him for a third time.

Tennessee native Olivia Reeves won gold in 71kg women's weightlifting, earning the U.S.'s first gold medal in the sport since the 2000 Sydney Olympics.

Reeves set an Olympic record by lifting 117 in her third snatch attempt.

The U.S. has now earned a medal on the women’s side for three CONSECUTIVE Olympic Games

Japanese breaker Ami Yuassa, known on the stage simply as Ami, won gold at the Olympic women's breaking final to claim the first Olympic gold medal in the sport's history.

Ami, 25, previously won gold at the 2022 World Games and silver at the 2022 Asian games.

Breaking was introduced to the Olympics this year. Lithuania's Nicka came in second to Ami, winning silver.

Minutes prior to the gold medal match, China's 671 won the first-ever medal in Olympic breakdancing, earning bronze.



American sprinter Christian Coleman was part of the mishandled baton pass that caused disqualification for the US during the men's 4x100 relay on Friday.

Coleman did not hand the baton off cleanly to teammate Kenny Bednarek. When Bednarek started leaving his mark, Coleman was nowhere near the tape in their lane.

Coleman actually ran a good leg, but Bednarek left way too early and gave them basically no shot to complete handoff cleanly.

"It's a little disappointing, especially for America, because we wanted to bring it home," Coleman told reporters after the race."We knew we had the speed to do it ... but we are human beings, too. We've been through ups and downs in life. This is just another one of those times where we just got to keep our head down and just keep pushing."

U.S. women's weightlifter Olivia Reeves set an Olympic record by lifting 117 in her third snatch attempt. She is a perfect 3-for-3 in her snatch session.

She is on pace to win gold heading into the clean and jerk lifts.

The U.S. last won a gold medal in women's weightlifting at the 2000 Sydney Olympics.



The U.S. was disqualified from the men's 4x100 relay after botching their first handoff, when Christian Coleman passed to Kenny Bednarek outside of the designated zone.

The U.S. men's team is historically notorious for fumbling relay handoffs.

The group of Coleman, Bednarek, Kyree King and Fred Kerley initially finished in seventh place as the fumbled handoff caused them to fall behind, and they never recovered.

Canada finished first at 37.50, South Africa took second, running a 37.57 and Great Britain crossed the line at 37.61 to take Bronze.



The U.S. did not medal in the men's 4x100 relay after a fumbled handoff.

Christian Coleman, Kenneth Bednarek, Kyree King and Fred Kerley suffered a disastrous baton fumble on their first pass and never recovered.

The U.S were the defending world champions, but were looking for their first Olympic gold in the event in 24 years, but fell short.

The American 100-meter gold medalist Noah Lyles was supposed to compete in this race, but had to withdraw after contracting COVID-19 on Wednesday. Kyree King competed in place for Lyles.

Canada took gold. USC alums Aaron Brown and Andre de Grasse helped lead Canada to its gold.



The U.S. won gold in the women's 4x100 relay, as Sha'Carri Richardson closed out the victory with a winning burst. The U.S. finished with a time of 41.78 seconds.

Richardson was joined by Gabby Thomas, Melissa Jefferson and Twanisha Terry in helping the U.S. clinch its 10th gold medal in track and field and 31st gold of Paris.

The U.S. overcame a a hiccup on the exchange between Thomas and Richardson and still ended up finishing in first over Great Britain for the silver and Germany for the bronze.

The gold marks an improvement on their silver medal relay finish in Tokyo. For Richardson, it is her first Olympic gold medal.



The U.S. women's basketball team defeated Australia in the semifinal 85-64 to advance to the gold medal game against either France or Belgium.

Breanna Stewart scored 16 points and Jackie Young scored 14 to lead the U.S.

The U.S. is looking for its eighth straight Olympic gold in women's basketball.



Team USA women’s basketball team leads Australia 45-27 after halftime in the semifinal game of the Paris Olympics on Friday.

Breanna Stewart and Jackie Young are leading the way for the Americans, scoring 11 and 10 points, respectively. Alanna Smith leads the scoring for Australia with seven points.

The United States took an early lead, but Australia managed to cut it to a four point lead, making it 18-14 in the first quarter. After that, Team USA went on a 27-13 run.

The winner of this game will play the winner of France vs. Belgium, who play at 3 p.m. ET on Friday. The gold medal game will be played on Sunday at 9:30 a.m. ET.



It wasn’t the gold medal that was on the line, but the Americans played like it was.

Team USA claimed its sixth Olympic medal in men’s volleyball after a dominating three set win over Italy in theParis Olympicsbronze medal match on Friday. The United States closed out the game with another close set, winning it all 25-23, 30-28, 26-24.

Friday’s victory in Paris marks the national team's third bronze medal and sixth overall. They previously won gold in 1984, 1988, and most recently in 2008.



The Americans are just one set away from reclaiming their spot on the podium after winning a thrilling second set over Italy in Friday’s bronze medal match.

It was Torey Defalco’s kill on the fourth set point that won the United States the second set, 30-28.

A win at theParis Olympicswould mark the United States' sixth Olympic medal, and third bronze medal.



The Americans are looking to get back on the podium at the2024 Paris Olympics,after an early and unexpected exit just three years ago in Tokyo.

Team USA claimed a narrow victory in the first set, winning it 25-23.

The now veteran squad failed to make it out of the group stages at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, after previously winning the bronze medal in the 2016 Rio Games. They last won gold at the 2008 Beijing Olympics, but failed to make it to the championship game in Paris after an upset loss to Poland in the semifinals.



The United States men’s water polo team is still in medal contention after suffering a 10-6 loss to Serbia in Friday’s semifinal game of the2024 Paris Olympics.

Team USA failed to find the back of the net in the fourth period as the two-time defending champions managed to extend their lead with two unanswered goals. The Americans will now have a chance to medal in Sunday’s bronze medal match against the winner of Hungary vs. Croatia.

The U.S. last medaled in this event in 2008 when they won the silver medal. They have never won a gold medal in men’swater polo.

Nelly Korda from the Eiffel Tower sinks it in for the birdie! Well, not exactly.

The American found the bottom of the cup from 82 yards out to birdie on the 7th hole atLe Golf Nationalon Friday.

Korda found herself in a little bit of trouble after a wayward drive, she was hoping to just put herself in position to scramble for a par. But after her incredible shot, she could keep the putter in the bag. The birdie brought her back to even par on the day, 2-under par for the tournament.

The 26-year-old looked like she was going to be at the top of the leaderboard Thursday. She had six birdies on the day after the 15th hole but proceeded to get a quad bogey on the 16th hole, and a bogey on the 17th.

The no. 1 ranked golfer in the world went from being 6-under and in sole possession of second place, to 2-under par after settling down to birdie the 18th hole, ending her second round in a tie for 12th.

Korda is the defendingOlympic champion.She won gold at the 2020 Tokyo Games.

Ryan Canfield contributed to this report.



The2024 Summer Olympicsin Paris is heating up with several track and field medal events on Friday, including the men’s and women’s 4x100m relay final. The men’s team is eyeing a gold medal despite track star Noah Lyles likely skipping out on the event after contracting COVID earlier in the week.

Team USA’s men’s volleyball team is also battling out against Italy in hopes of medaling in the bronze medal match, while the men’s water polo team plays in their first semifinals game since 2008.

Here is a list of must-watch events on Friday, August 9:

Men’s water polo semifinal (USA vs. Serbia): 8:35 a.m. ET

Men’s volleyball bronze medal match (USA vs. Italy): 10 a.m. ET

Women’s basketball semifinal (USA vs. Australia): 11:30 a.m. ET

Women’s 4x100 relay final: 1:30 p.m. ET

Women’s shot put final: 1:37 p.m. ET

Men’s 4x100 relay final: 1:47 p.m. ET

Women’s 400m final: 2:00 p.m. ET

Men’s triple jump final: 2:13 p.m. ET

Women’s 10,000m final: 2:57 p.m. ET

Men’s 400m hurdles final: 3:45 p.m. ET



American track star Noah Lyles earned a bronze medal in the men's 200-meter final on Thursday, despite entering the race as the overwhelming favorite. Already agold medalist in Parisafter winning the men's 100-meter sprint, Lyles revealed after the race that he contracted COVID days earlier.

But his result in Thursday's final might be his last medal of these Summer Games.

"I believe this will be the end of my 2024 Olympics. It is not the Olympic I dreamed of but it has left me with so much Joy in my heart," Lyles wrote in an Instagram post. "I hope everyone enjoyed the show. Whether you were rooting for me or against me, you have to admit you watched, didn’t you? See you next time."

The men's 4x100 team relay final is slated to begin Friday at 1:47 p.m. ET, but Lyles had told NBC's broadcast after the 200m that the rest of the 4x100 team "can handle it without me."

title: Day 14 of 2024 Paris Olympics concludes with Team USA leading the medal count | Live Updates from Fox News Digital
description: Team USA continues to lead at the 2024 Paris Olympics with 111 total medals at the end of day 14. The U.S. is tied with China for the most gold medals with 33.
keywords: news, breaking news, latest news, current news, world news, national news
